task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the correct patient record for Matt Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should display a patient record or evaluation screen for 'Matt Zenon Betz'.

task_completion.description: Verify an active diagnosis for corneal foreign body was added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A patient condition or diagnosis entry showing ICD-10 code 'T15' or 'T15.0' (Foreign body on external eye).

task_completion.description: Verify a clinical evaluation was created with an elevated heart rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation screen showing the Heart Rate (HR) field filled with a value of 90 or greater.

task_completion.description: Verify the Chief Complaint or Notes document the safety failure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Chief Complaint or Notes section of the clinical evaluation contains the word 'PPE' or 'metal'.

task_completion.description: Verify a prescription order was created with at least two distinct medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription screen for the patient showing a single order with at least two separate medication lines/items.

task_completion.description: Verify a follow-up appointment was scheduled for 1 to 2 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduling screen showing Matt Zenon Betz with an appointment date set to 1 or 2 days after the current system date visible in the OS/taskbar.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client windows, menus, and forms being actively used, rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor or mock application to simulate task completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must clearly be the Tryton/GNU Health client, with standard icons, tabs, and window borders.

integrity.description: Verify the medications were added to a single prescription order, not multiple separate orders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The two or more medications should be listed as lines within the same prescription document window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target patient's full name in the database is 'Matt Zenon Betz' (born 2010). The required ICD-10 code is T15 or T15.0. The heart rate must be >= 90. The evaluation notes or chief complaint must contain 'PPE' or 'metal'. The prescription must contain at least 2 distinct medications. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled exactly 1 to 2 days from the current system date.
